findings.summary: Codex runtime agent docs still point to `.md` files even though the Codex runtime contract and live agent files use `.toml`.
findings.recommended_fix: Normalize all active Codex runtime references to `.codex/agents/*.toml` and add a parity guard that rejects `.md` references in Codex runtime docs.

findings.summary: OpenCode, Codex, and Gemini runtime README indexes still list deleted `handover` and `speckit` agents.
findings.recommended_fix: Regenerate or hand-edit the stale runtime README indexes from the current directory contents so deleted agents cannot be advertised as live.

findings.summary: `@general` is treated as a directory-routed agent in AGENTS but as a built-in in runtime docs, leaving no single authoritative definition model.
findings.recommended_fix: Either document `@general` as explicitly built-in everywhere or add a dedicated runtime-backed definition and include it in agent-file tables.

findings.summary: Deep-research and deep-review mode suffix syntax is inconsistent across AGENTS, skill docs, quick references, and command cards.
findings.recommended_fix: Choose one suffix syntax in the parser contract and update every published example to match it.

findings.summary: Deep-research quick-reference convergence weights disagree with the detailed convergence reference.
findings.recommended_fix: Make one convergence document authoritative and generate or prune the duplicated weight table from the other surface.

findings.summary: Child-phase deep-research runs use a split artifact layout: docs promise `{spec_folder}/research`, the resolver writes state into track-root `research/{phaseSlug}-pt-{NN}`, and prompt files still write phase-local.
findings.recommended_fix: Route prompts, state, and synthesis through the same resolved artifact directory and update the state-format docs to match the live contract.

findings.summary: Distributed-governance rules for markdown writes are not enforced by the deep-research workflow that owns `research/research.md`.
findings.recommended_fix: Add explicit deep-research validation hooks or document a formal exemption for research-owned markdown outputs.

findings.summary: Workflow assets treat the OpenCode deep-loop agent copies as canonical while AGENTS tells operators to stay inside the active runtime directory.
findings.recommended_fix: Document the source-generation model for deep-loop agents or align the workflow assets with the runtime-local loading rule.

findings.summary: Phase 002 completion claims overstate the live cleanup state because active runtime docs still expose stale agent surfaces.
findings.recommended_fix: Track the remaining runtime-surface cleanup in a follow-up packet or reopen phase 002 so closeout evidence matches the current tree.
